concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: LIFO
concepts.short_definition: The principle where the last element added to a structure is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: A logical description of data and a set of operations that can be performed on that data, independent of implementation.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer so that it can be accessed and modified efficiently.

concepts.name: Push Operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop Operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing and returning the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek Operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of viewing the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: Empty Stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack that contains no elements.

concepts.name: Full Stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack that has reached its maximum capacity (relevant for fixed-size implementations).

concepts.name: Top Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that keeps track of the index or position of the top element in the stack.

concepts.name: Underflow
concepts.short_definition: An error condition that occurs when attempting to pop or peek from an empty stack.

concepts.name: Overflow
concepts.short_definition: An error condition that occurs when attempting to push an element onto a full stack (relevant for fixed-size implementations).

concepts.name: Array Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a stack using an array as the underlying storage mechanism.

concepts.name: Linked List Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a stack using a linked list as the underlying storage mechanism.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored contiguously in memory; each element points to the next.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A basic unit in a linked list, typically containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the execution time of an algorithm grows as the input size increases.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the memory usage of an algorithm grows as the input size increases.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time does not depend on the size of the input.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows linearly with the size of the input.

concepts.name: Applications of Stack
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and problems where stacks are effectively used.

concepts.name: Function Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: A mechanism used by programming languages to manage function calls and their local variables.

concepts.name: Expression Evaluation
concepts.short_definition: Using stacks to convert and evaluate mathematical expressions.

concepts.name: Backtracking Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms that explore possibilities and undo choices, often using stacks.

concepts.name: Undo/Redo Functionality
concepts.short_definition: Implementing undo and redo features in applications using stacks.

concepts.name: Syntax Parsing
concepts.short_definition: Using stacks to analyze the grammatical structure of code.

concepts.name: Data Storage
concepts.short_definition: How stacks manage temporary data storage during program execution.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself.

concepts.name: Base Case
concepts.short_definition: The condition in a recursive function that stops the recursion.

concepts.name: Recursive Step
concepts.short_definition: The part of a recursive function that breaks down the problem and calls itself.

concepts.name: Stack Frame
concepts.short_definition: A block of memory on the call stack that stores information about a function call.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of assigning memory to data during program execution.

concepts.name: Scope
concepts.short_definition: The region of a program where a variable is accessible.

concepts.name: Return Address
concepts.short_definition: The memory location to which control returns after a function call completes.

concepts.name: Local Variables
concepts.short_definition: Variables declared within a function that are only accessible within that function.

concepts.name: Parameters
concepts.short_definition: Values passed into a function when it is called.

concepts.name: Infix Notation
concepts.short_definition: Mathematical notation where operators appear between operands (e.g., a + b).

concepts.name: Postfix Notation
concepts.short_definition: Mathematical notation where operators appear after operands (e.g., ab+).

concepts.name: Prefix Notation
concepts.short_definition: Mathematical notation where operators appear before operands (e.g., +ab).
